title: Manyzz Texture
description: a high-resolution resource pack that brings lifelike textures to every block and biome.
body:
# **🌾 Manyzz Texture Pack (Java Edition) – 128x Realistic**

Manyzz Texture Pack (Java Edition) brings a clean, high-resolution 128x realistic style to Minecraft while keeping the original atmosphere intact.
This version is still in development and does not yet contain all textures included in the Bedrock Edition, but it already includes a strong foundation of high-quality overworld blocks.

# **✨ What’s Included (Java Version)**

The Java version currently contains:

- Sand + Sandstone family
  
- Red Sand + Red Sandstone family
  
- All overworld woods (oak, spruce, dark oak, jungle, acacia, mangrove, cherry, pale)
  
- Leaves, doors, trapdoors
  
- Overworld ores + deepslate ores
  
- All deepslate variants
  
- Essential building blocks
  
These textures are fully remade in 128x resolution with smooth shading and realistic details without breaking the vanilla style.

# **⚠️ Note: Differences from Bedrock Version**

The **Bedrock Edition v2.0** contains much more content.
After analyzing both packs:

### **🔥 The Java version is missing 555 textures**

These textures are already available in the Bedrock version but not yet ported to Java.

### **📘 Examples of what’s still missing in Java:**

- **Plants & crops:** azalea leaves, roses, beetroot stages, bamboo, kelp
  
- **Nether blocks:** ancient debris, warped & crimson wood families
  
- **Utility blocks:** anvil variants, beehive, smithing table, campfire
  
- **End blocks:** purpur, end stone variants
  
- **Workstations:** loom, grindstone, stonecutter, cartography table
  
- **Redstone components:** comparators, observers, pistons, hoppers

These categories make up the remaining **555 textures** still needed to catch up to Bedrock v2.0.

# **🎯 Goal of the Java Edition**

To fully match the **Manyzz Bedrock Edition**, delivering:

- 100% texture parity
  
- Same realism quality
  
- Same block coverage
  
- Optimized performance for Java players
  
Future updates will continue adding more missing textures until both versions are completely synchronized.
